title: kunzea ericifolia subsp. subulata
intro: Kunzea ericifolia subsp. subulata, also known as Tasmanian kunzea or Tasmanian paperbark, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the myrtle family, Myrtaceae. This plant is valued for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues. The health benefits of Kunzea ericifolia subsp. subulata include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. The plant's medicinal properties can be prepared in various forms, including essential oils, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
